<<Transforming JSON to Python Dictionary>>

SECTION 1  
The JSON data provided contains three key-value pairs: "name", "age", and "city". These represent individual details such as the full legal name, age in years, and the urban area where the person resides. The task is to convert this JSON structure into a Python dictionary.  

SECTION 2  
The resulting Python dictionary will have the same keys as the JSON but with the values converted to appropriate data types. The "name" key will be a string, "age" will be an integer, and "city" will be a string. The dictionary will be self-contained and adhere to the specified formatting rules, including proper punctuation and capitalization.
